2606.13607.title: Reasoning as Pattern Matching: Shared Mechanisms in Human and LLM Everyday Reasoning
2606.13607.paper_summary: When large language models (LLMs) fail to generalize or make haphazard errors in reasoning, it is often taken as evidence that LLMs are not truly reasoning, but rather performing a kind of pattern matching. The implication is that people's behavior does not exhibit the same types of failures because human reasoning uses principled and abstract world models. We evaluate human participants and 25 LLMs on their ability to engage in common-sense reasoning about a variety of everyday situations and observe similar patterns of errors in both people and models. We then identify the set of attention heads driving LLM responses and find that these heads implement a form of pattern-matching. These attention heads allow us to predict seemingly inexplicable reasoning errors in people caused by ostensibly irrelevant prompt details. Taken together, our results suggest that everyday causal reasoning in people and LLMs is more consistent with a form of pattern-matching than with abstract world models.
2606.13607.paper_summary_zh: 當大型語言模型 (LLMs) 無法進行概括或在推理中出現隨意錯誤時，這通常被視為證據，表明 LLMs 並不真正進行推理，而是執行一種模式匹配。這意味著人類的行為並不會表現出相同類型的失誤，因為人類推理使用的是有原則和抽象的世界模型。我們評估了人類參與者和 25 個 LLM 在日常情境中進行常識推理的能力，並觀察到人類和模型中出現相似的錯誤模式。然後，我們識別出驅動 LLM 回應的一組注意力頭，並發現這些頭實現了一種模式匹配的形式。這些注意力頭使我們能夠預測人類中由表面上無關的提示細節引起的看似無法解釋的推理錯誤。綜合來看，我們的結果表明，人類和 LLM 的日常因果推理更符合一種模式匹配的形式，而不是抽象的世界模型。
2606.13607.author: Zach Studdiford et.al.
